bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2031?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2031" sits ahead of the FTM AGI-training-threshold curve's earliest priced year (2033); used as nearest available anchor, so timing offset weakens the tie. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2032?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2032" is one year ahead of the nearest priced FTM AGI-threshold year (2033), a close timing match. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2033?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2033" nearly coincides with the FTM AGI-training-threshold-by-2033 variable. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2034?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2034" directly corresponds to the FTM AGI-training-requirements-met-by-2033 threshold (one-year lead convention). [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2035?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2035" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2034 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2036?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2036" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2035 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2037?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2037" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2036 threshold. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2036': 'ftm_agi_by_2035'}]

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2038?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2038" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2037 threshold. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2037': 'ftm_agi_by_2038'}]

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2039?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2039" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2038 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2041?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2041" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2040 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2042?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2042" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2041 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2043?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2043" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2042 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2044?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2044" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2043 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2045?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2045" directly corresponds to FTM's AGI-by-2044 threshold.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2046?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2046" is essentially FTM's AGI-by-2045 threshold (last priced year).

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2048?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2048" extends past FTM's last priced AGI year (2045); used as the ceiling anchor, timing offset limits tightness.

bridges.title: Will we get AGI before 2047?
bridges.rationale: "AGI before 2047" extends past FTM's last priced AGI year (2045); used as ceiling anchor.

bridges.title: Will AGI be achieved in the next 5 years?
bridges.rationale: "AGI in the next 5 years" (from 2026) implies ~2031, ahead of FTM's earliest priced AGI year (2033); weaker timing tie. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will artificial superintelligence exist by 2030?  [resolves N/A in 2027]
bridges.rationale: ASI-by-2030 is a stronger capability bar than the FTM AGI-training threshold, but AGI is a near-prerequisite for ASI, so treated as a leading indicator with reduced strength. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will the ARC AGI Grand Prize be claimed before January 2027?
bridges.rationale: Claiming the ARC-AGI grand prize is a general-reasoning capability milestone that would plausibly precede or coincide with FTM's AGI training threshold. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will the ARC-AGI grand prize be claimed by end of 2026?
bridges.rationale: Same ARC-AGI grand-prize logic as x0112; independent external market, same nearest-year FTM anchor. [parent remapped: {'ftm_agi_by_2033': 'ftm_agi_by_2034'}]

bridges.title: Will models be able to do the work of an AI researcher/engineer before 2027?
bridges.rationale: "Models do AI-researcher-level work before 2027" is essentially FTM's definition of the 5%-R&D-automation threshold by 2027.

bridges.title: Will AI Research Be Mostly Autonomous By June 1 2027?
bridges.rationale: "AI research mostly autonomous by June 2027" is a stronger bar than FTM's 5% R&D-automation threshold but the same underlying metric and horizon.

bridges.title: Will AI be Recursively Self Improving by mid 2026?
bridges.rationale: Recursive self-improvement by mid-2026 is conceptually the leading edge of R&D task automation; nearest priced FTM year is 2027.

bridges.title: METR 80% time horizon exceeds 10h before September
bridges.rationale: METR's 80%-reliable task-horizon exceeding 10h is an empirical proxy for the same R&D-automation progress FTM's auto_rnd metric tracks.

bridges.title: SOAI#3: Open-ended agents make a meaningful scientific discovery end-to-end (hypothesis, expt, iteration, paper).
bridges.rationale: Open-ended end-to-end scientific discovery by agents is a mid-tier R&D-automation milestone; no explicit resolution year, so mapped to a mid-horizon FTM anchor.

bridges.title: 1-hour AGI.  A system capable of any computer-based task a human can do in 1 hour will be developed by end of 2026.
bridges.rationale: A system doing any 1-hour human computer task is a concrete, near-term capability/automation-horizon bar close to FTM's 5%-R&D-automation-by-2027 threshold.

bridges.title: As predicted by AI 2027, will AI surpass all living humans in coding ability by January 2027?
bridges.rationale: AI surpassing all humans at coding by Jan 2027 is essentially a coding-specific instance of FTM's R&D-task-automation threshold.

bridges.title: Will AI surpass humans in conducting scientific research by 2030?
bridges.rationale: AI surpassing humans in scientific research by 2030 aligns with FTM's 20%-R&D-automation-by-2030 threshold.

bridges.title: Will AI be capable of producing an Annals-quality math paper for $100k by March 2030?
bridges.rationale: Producing an Annals-quality math paper is a research-capability milestone loosely tracking FTM's R&D-automation curve; underlying threshold tier is uncertain.

bridges.title: Will AI cause the US Unemployment Rate to exceed 10% before 2030?
bridges.rationale: AI pushing US unemployment above 10% by 2030 is a direct consequence of FTM's 20%-goods-automation-by-2030 threshold being crossed.

bridges.title: Will AI cause mass unemployment... by 2030 ?
bridges.rationale: "AI causes mass unemployment by 2030" tracks the same 20%-goods-task-automation threshold.

bridges.title: Will >50% of human physical labor be replaced by robots before 2030?
bridges.rationale: 50%+ of physical labor replaced by robots by 2030 is a strong-automation scenario consistent with FTM's 20%-goods-automation-by-2030 threshold being met.

bridges.title: Will we have near-complete automation of the world economy before 2035?
bridges.rationale: "Near-complete automation of the world economy before 2035" is essentially FTM's full-automation-of-goods-and-services-by-2035 variable.

bridges.title: Will we have near-complete automation of the world economy before 2030?
bridges.rationale: "Near-complete automation before 2030" precedes FTM's earliest priced full-automation year (2033); used as nearest anchor.

bridges.title: Will >97% of all jobs be fully automated before the year 2075?
bridges.rationale: 97% job automation by 2075 is a much longer horizon than FTM's last priced full-automation year (2045); used as a directional (not tight) anchor.

bridges.title: Will more than half the US population actively work with AI daily by 2027?
bridges.rationale: Half the US population using AI daily by 2027 is an adoption proxy loosely upstream of FTM's 5%-goods-automation threshold; nearest priced year used.

bridges.title: Will a country ban human driven cars in favor of AI self driving in at least one city center by the end of 2040?
bridges.rationale: A country banning human-driven cars in favor of AI by 2040 presumes broad task automation consistent with FTM's 20%-goods-automation-by-2040 threshold.

bridges.title: Will most digital entertainment be AI generated by 2040?
bridges.rationale: Most digital entertainment being AI-generated by 2040 is a sectoral instance of FTM's broader goods-and-services automation threshold.

bridges.title: In early 2028, will an AI be able to generate a full high-quality movie to a prompt?
bridges.rationale: AI generating a full high-quality movie by early 2028 is a creative-task-automation milestone loosely within FTM's goods-and-services automation frame; high forecaster count (4497) justifies inclusion despite a loose metric match.

bridges.title: Will global corp spending on AI be ≥$700B during the calendar year 2029?
bridges.rationale: Global corporate AI spend >=$700B in 2029 is essentially FTM's compute-investment-exceeds-1%-of-world-output-by-2029 threshold.

bridges.title: $100B AI training cluster before 2029?
bridges.rationale: A $100B training cluster before 2029 is a concrete instance of compute investment crossing FTM's 1%-of-world-output-by-2029 threshold.

bridges.title: At least one of the most powerful neural nets at end of 2026 will be trained using 10^27 FLOPs
bridges.rationale: A 10^27 FLOP training run by end-2026 is one order of magnitude below FTM's smallest priced training-run threshold (1e28 by 2027); used as the nearest larger-threshold anchor.

bridges.title: 2030 – 3. Over one hundred thousand humanoid robots will be deployed in the real world.
bridges.rationale: 100k+ humanoid robots deployed by 2030 depends on hardware cost-performance gains FTM tracks via hw_ratio; nearest priced year (2032) used since 2030 isn't priced in that family.

bridges.title: Will OpenAI, DeepMind, or Anthropic have revenue of at least $100B in 2027?
bridges.rationale: Frontier labs reaching $100B revenue by 2027 is a downstream indicator of the compute/AI-investment scale FTM's gwp_compute metric tracks.

bridges.title: GPT-6 released in 2026?
bridges.rationale: A GPT-6-class release in 2026 is a proxy for continued frontier-scaling pace, loosely tied to FTM's training-run-scale threshold (nearest priced year 2027).

bridges.title: Will there be a period of 12 contiguous months during which no new compute-SOTA LM is released, by Jan 1, 2033?
bridges.rationale: A 12-month stretch with no new compute-SOTA model by 2033 is the direct negation of continued large-training-run progress FTM's train_run metric tracks.

bridges.title: Are we about to hit another AI winter in 2026?
bridges.rationale: An AI winter starting in 2026 is the inverse of continued training-run scaling; nearest priced FTM year (2027) used.

bridges.title: By EOY 2026, will it seem as if deep learning hit a wall by EOY 2025?
bridges.rationale: "Deep learning hit a wall by end-2025/judged by end-2026" is a direct stall indicator opposing FTM's training-run-scaling curve.

bridges.title: Will the AI bubble pop in 2026?
bridges.rationale: An AI investment bubble popping in 2026 would suppress compute investment as a share of world output, the inverse of FTM's gwp_compute threshold.

bridges.title: Will AI be life-changing for muggles by the end of 2029?
bridges.rationale: "AI life-changing for ordinary people by 2029" is a diffuse adoption/impact claim loosely upstream of FTM's 5%-goods-automation-by-2029 threshold.

bridges.title: 5Y after AGI, universal basic income?
bridges.rationale: "5 years after AGI, UBI?" requires AGI to have occurred within the operative horizon; FTM's AGI-by-2040 threshold (mid-horizon) is used as the enabling-condition proxy.

bridges.title: 5Y after AGI, nuclear fusion 10% world energy
bridges.rationale: "5 years after AGI, does fusion reach 10% of world energy?" likewise presupposes AGI timing; FTM's AGI-by-2040 threshold used as enabling-condition proxy.

bridges.title: Will fully autonomous (level 5) self-driving cars be available in a major US city before 2030?
bridges.rationale: Level-5 self-driving availability by 2030 plausibly needs both broad task-automation progress and cheaper AI-hardware price-performance; treated as two semi-independent FTM drivers.

bridges.title: By 2030, will general-purpose robot learning new tasks be >$20k?
bridges.rationale: Robot learning-new-tasks cost falling under $20k by 2030 depends on both hardware price-performance and software/algorithmic efficiency gains -- two largely orthogonal FTM mechanisms.

bridges.title: By 2028, will there be a visible break in trend line on US GDP, GDP per capita, unemployment, or productivity, which most economists attribu
bridges.rationale: A visible AI-attributed break in US macro trend lines by 2028 is jointly driven by aggregate growth acceleration and goods/services task automation, two distinct FTM metrics.
